issue.body:
## Summary

`tests/ocr/test_reorganize_page_utils_grouping.py::test_reorganize_page_expected_text_outputs` flaked under `pytest -n auto` with a `FileNotFoundError` while writing to `tests/fixtures/layout_regression/expected_text/current/<case>.reorganize.txt`. The parent directory disappeared between the test's `mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)` (line 264) and the `write_text` call (line 326). One specific parametrize case (`plate-chairs-beauvais-tapestry`) failed; the other 25 + 5 xfail in the same sweep all succeeded. A subsequent re-run on the same SHA passed cleanly. Hence: not deterministically broken — **flaky under parallel execution**.

## Traceback

```
FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '.../tests/fixtures/layout_regression/expected_text/current/plate-chairs-beauvais-tapestry.reorganize.txt'

File "...pathlib/_local.py", line 555, in write_text
File "...pathlib/_abc.py", line 651, in write_text
File ".../io.open(self, mode, buffering, encoding, errors, newline)
```

The failure is `mode='w'` failing on `write_text` — the parent dir vanished after the per-test `mkdir`.

## Reproducibility

- Hit **once** in a 31-case parametrize sweep on `master @ 57eeeda`.
- Direct re-run of the same `pytest -n auto` invocation on the same SHA: all 26 cases pass.
- No reproducer; appears to be a timing-dependent xdist race.

## Suspected root cause

The test module currently uses module-level shared dirs across all parametrizations:

```python
TEXT_CURRENT_DIR = FIXTURE_ROOT / "expected_text" / "current"
TEXT_DIFF_DIR    = FIXTURE_ROOT / "expected_text" / "diff"
```

A session-scoped fixture (lines 183–184) wipes those dirs at session start with `shutil.rmtree(..., ignore_errors=True)`. The fixture's docstring already acknowledges the xdist race on the rmtree itself — but under `pytest-xdist`, **each worker process runs its own session-scoped setup**, so worker A's session-start `rmtree` can fire after worker B has already entered a test, mkdir'd the dir, and is about to write into it. That window is small but nonzero, and it's consistent with the observed symptom (mkdir succeeded, write failed with parent-not-found).

The per-test `mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)` at line 264 does not protect against this — by the time `write_text` is called at line 326 the dir may have been rmtree'd from underneath by another worker.

## Impact

Causes false-bounce of `ship-issue` cycles when an unrelated slice's test run happens to flake on this parametrize. Surfaced during the workspace ship-issue pilot (Plan B / B5) — bot's actual slice for issue #11 was correct; this flake forced manual recovery.

## Likely fix paths

1. **Per-worker output dirs (cleanest).** Use `tmp_path` or a `PYTEST_XDIST_WORKER`-namespaced subdir for `current/` writes (the test already does this for `DEBUG_DIR` — see line 133 docstring), and only mirror to a shared canonical dir at session teardown. Eliminates cross-worker races by construction.
2. **Serialize the parametrize on one worker.** Add `@pytest.mark.xdist_group("layout_regression")` to the test (requires `pytest-xdist >= 3.x` and `--dist loadgroup`). Cheapest correctness fix; gives up some parallelism on this one test.
3. **Retry-on-FileNotFoundError (band-aid).** Wrap the write in a single retry that re-mkdirs and re-writes. Hides the root cause and leaves the rmtree-race window in place — recommend against unless 1 and 2 are infeasible.

## Out of scope

- Fixing pytest-xdist's session-fixture-per-worker behavior itself.
- The `ship-issue` orchestrator's response to flaky-test bounces — tracked separately at the workspace level.
issue.title: Flaky FileNotFoundError on `current/<case>.reorganize.txt` write under `pytest -n auto`

issue.body:
## Summary

Closes #14.

`test_reorganize_page_expected_text_outputs` flaked under `pytest -n auto` with `FileNotFoundError` while writing `current/<case>.reorganize.txt`. The parent dir disappeared between the test's per-test `mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)` and the subsequent `write_text` call.

## Root cause

The module had a session-scoped autouse fixture (`_wipe_text_outputs`) that did `shutil.rmtree(TEXT_CURRENT_DIR, ignore_errors=True)` at session start, where `TEXT_CURRENT_DIR` was a shared module-level path. Under `pytest-xdist`, each worker runs its own session-scoped setup, so worker A's session-start rmtree could fire **after** worker B had already entered a parametrized test, mkdir'd the dir, and was about to write into it. The window is small but nonzero — exactly consistent with the observed one-off flake on `plate-chairs-beauvais-tapestry`.

Diagnosis matches the issue body's "fix path 1" (per-worker output dirs) — closely related to the existing pattern in `_new_run_dir` for `DEBUG_DIR` (lines 137–148).

## Fix (path a from the issue body)

Scope each xdist worker's writes to a per-worker subtree by folding `PYTEST_XDIST_WORKER` into `TEXT_CURRENT_DIR` and `TEXT_DIFF_DIR`:

- Under xdist: `current/<gwN>/<case>.reorganize.txt` — each worker's session-start rmtree only nukes its own subtree, so cross-worker races become impossible by construction.
- Sequential (no xdist, `PYTEST_XDIST_WORKER` unset): writes go directly under the canonical root — legacy layout preserved.

The reads from these dirs are write-only artifacts for human debugging — the actual assertion compares an in-memory string against the baseline file — so per-worker isolation is safe.

## Why path (a) and not (b) or serialize?

- Path (a) is one well-localized change to two module-level constants + the existing rmtree. Sequential behaviour is unchanged.
- Path (b) (skip rmtree under xdist) leaves stale data from a prior xdist run in workers' subtrees on the next run.
- `xdist_group` requires `--dist loadgroup` and bottlenecks the slowest single test on one worker.

## Verification

- `uv run pytest -n auto -k test_reorganize_page_expected_text_outputs` x5 consecutive runs: 26 passed + 5 xfailed each time. Race did not reproduce.
- Sequential `uv run pytest -k ...`: 26 passed + 5 xfailed; canonical layout (`current/<case>.reorganize.txt`) preserved.
- Confirmed per-worker subdirs (`gw0..gw13`) are created under xdist.
- `make lint`: clean.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI green on this branch.
- [ ] Spot-check that no test still references the bare `current/` path expecting non-worker-scoped layout.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
issue.title: fix(test): per-worker current/diff subdirs to close xdist rmtree race

issue.body:
Filed from the pd-book-tools deep code/security review.

Report: `docs/research/2026-05-22-deep-code-security-review.md`
Finding: #14

Moved from mistakenly filed meta issue: ConcaveTrillion/ocr-container-meta#307

Evidence:

- `pd_book_tools/ocr/layout_aware_reorg.py:840`
- `pd_book_tools/ocr/layout_aware_reorg.py:846`
- `pd_book_tools/ocr/layout_aware_reorg.py:879`

Each illustration region independently calls `caption_for_figure()`. Two nearby illustration regions can select the same caption words; words are purged once, then emitted multiple times as new caption blocks.

Impact:

- Downstream text/layout output can duplicate captions.

Remediation:

- Track consumed caption regions or word IDs.
- Assign each caption to one nearest or most-overlapping illustration.

---
This issue was filed from a read-only review. Confirm behavior with a focused failing test before implementation where practical.
issue.title: pd-book-tools: caption association can duplicate captions across nearby figures
